description:
**Problem**
The realtime code client_request_id_reuse_mismatch exists but cannot be emitted. WAL index upsert overwrites existing (namespace, origin_replica_id, client_request_id) rows, hiding mismatched reuse and mutating history.

**Where**
- src/core/error.rs: ErrorCode::ClientRequestIdReuseMismatch (defined)
- src/daemon/ops.rs: OpError lacks a variant
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: upsert_client_request uses ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE

**Design**
- Add OpError variant and IPC mapping for client_request_id_reuse_mismatch with details {namespace, client_request_id, expected_request_sha256, got_request_sha256}.
- Change index behavior to preserve first-seen mapping; detect mismatch and return explicit error instead of overwriting.
- Keep idempotent reuse path returning original txn/event ids (no mutation) when request_sha matches.

**Acceptance**
- Reusing client_request_id with different request_sha256 returns client_request_id_reuse_mismatch (retryable=false).
- WAL index no longer overwrites request_sha256/txn_id/event_ids on conflict.
- Matching reuse returns prior ids without mutating history.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs
- src/core/error.rs
title: Idempotency: detect client_request_id reuse mismatch

acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- AddDep rejects DepKind::Parent at parse boundary.
- SetParent is the only way to create parent edges, and it removes prior parent edges.
- Tests confirm multiple parents cannot be introduced via any mutation path.
description:
Problem
- ParsedMutationRequest::AddDep allows DepKind::Parent.
- This bypasses the SetParent path and can create multiple parents for a bead.
- The "single parent" invariant exists only in call-site discipline; the compiler can't enforce it.

Impact
- Tree semantics for hierarchy can be broken silently.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and introduces "lies" (API implies any dep kind is valid).
design:
Design
- Remove Parent from the AddDep surface: either reject DepKind::Parent in parse_add_dep or expose a distinct request type for parents only.
- Encode parent edges as a dedicated operation with its own validated type (e.g., ParentEdgeAdd { child, parent, proof: NoCycleProof }).
- Optionally encode in DepKind by splitting Parent into a distinct enum used only in SetParent.
- Ensure apply path guarantees at most one Parent edge (remove existing parent before add, as plan_set_parent does).

Scatter fit
- Parent invariants live in one operation; other dep ops cannot express Parent at all.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- crates/beads-core/src/dep.rs
- crates/beads-core/src/domain.rs (if splitting enum)
- crates/beads-core/src/event.rs (validation)
title: mutation: forbid AddDep with Parent; encode single-parent invariant

description: Many fixtures spawn git init/config/remote for temp repos (critical_path, migration, daemon fixtures, repl_rig). Consider a shared helper that uses git2 APIs to init repo, set config, and add remote without process spawn. Should speed test setup and reduce dependency on git binary.
title: Use libgit2 for test repo setup to avoid spawning git
